TopModule.demo_behavior:
Module: TopModule I would like you to implement a module named TopModule with the following interface. All input and output ports are one bit unless otherwise specified. - input  c - input  d - output mux_in (4 bits) For the following Karnaugh map, give the circuit implementation using one 4-to-1 multiplexer and as many 2-to-1 multiplexers as required, but using as few as possible. You are not allowed to use any other logic gate and you must use _a_ and _b_ as the multiplexer selector inputs, as shown on the 4-to-1 multiplexer below. ab cd  00  01  11  10 00 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 01 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 11 | 1 | 0 | 1 | 1 | 10 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 1 | Consider a block diagram with inputs 'c' and 'd' going into a module called "TopModule". This "TopModule" has four outputs, mux_in[3:0], that connect to a four input mux. The mux takes as input {a,b} and ab = 00 is connected to mux_in[0], ab=01 is connected to mux_in[1], and so in. You are implementing in Verilog just the portion labelled "TopModule", such that the entire circuit (including the 4-to-1 mux) implements the K-map.
Key rules: Implement behavior exactly as described in the prompt.
Reset: Only as explicitly described in the prompt.
Corner cases: No additional corner cases beyond prompt requirements.
Performance: Use prompt-defined timing/latency behavior.

test_goals: Pass benchmark harness checks for all provided tests.
verification.oracle_strategy: Use benchmark-provided reference outputs as oracle.
pass_fail_criteria: DUT outputs match oracle outputs for benchmark tests.
